Bulk RNA-seq Deconvolution Benchmark

Source: vignettes/BulkDeconvolution.Rmd
BulkDeconvolution.Rmd

This tutorial benchmarks SpaCET.deconvolution.bulk() and SpaCET.deconvolution.matched.scRNAseq() using pseudobulk mixtures from real scRNA-seq data with a fair subject-level train/test split.

Design: Wu et al. 2021 (Nature Genetics, GSE176078) BRCA scRNA-seq is split 50/50 by patient. The TRAIN set provides the deconvolution reference; pseudobulk is generated from the TEST set only. This ensures no cell overlap between reference and evaluation data.

Four scenarios:

  1. Uniform mixing (Dirichlet alpha=1.0)
  2. Sparse mixing (Dirichlet alpha=0.3)
  3. Tumor purity (60-90% malignant)
  4. Titration (0-90% malignant)

Setup 

library(SpaCET)
library(Matrix)

Step 1: Load scRNA-seq Data

Download Wu et al. 2021 BRCA scRNA-seq from GEO (GSE176078). We expect a count matrix and cell metadata with columns celltype_major and orig.ident (patient/subject ID).

# Load preprocessed scRNA-seq (adjust path to your data)
# Expected format: counts (genes x cells), metadata with cell_type and subject_id
data_path <- "data/BRCA_scRNA"

sc_counts <- readRDS(file.path(data_path, "counts.rds"))       # genes x cells sparse matrix
sc_meta   <- read.csv(file.path(data_path, "metadata.csv"),
                       row.names = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Standardize column names
if ("celltype_major" %in% colnames(sc_meta) && !"cell_type" %in% colnames(sc_meta)) {
  sc_meta$cell_type <- sc_meta$celltype_major
}
if ("orig.ident" %in% colnames(sc_meta) && !"subject_id" %in% colnames(sc_meta)) {
  sc_meta$subject_id <- sc_meta$orig.ident
}

cat(sprintf("Loaded: %d cells x %d genes, %d subjects\n",
            ncol(sc_counts), nrow(sc_counts),
            length(unique(sc_meta$subject_id))))
cat("Cell types:\n")
print(table(sc_meta$cell_type))

Step 2: Subject-Level Train/Test Split 

set.seed(42)
subjects <- sort(unique(sc_meta$subject_id))
subjects <- sample(subjects)  # shuffle
mid <- length(subjects) %/% 2

train_subjects <- subjects[1:mid]
test_subjects  <- subjects[(mid + 1):length(subjects)]

train_mask <- sc_meta$subject_id %in% train_subjects
test_mask  <- sc_meta$subject_id %in% test_subjects

sc_counts_train <- sc_counts[, train_mask]
sc_meta_train   <- sc_meta[train_mask, ]

sc_counts_test  <- sc_counts[, test_mask]
sc_meta_test    <- sc_meta[test_mask, ]

cat(sprintf("Train: %d cells, %d subjects\n", ncol(sc_counts_train), length(train_subjects)))
cat(sprintf("Test:  %d cells, %d subjects\n", ncol(sc_counts_test), length(test_subjects)))

Step 3: Generate Pseudobulk from TEST Set

Helpers 

#' Generate pseudobulk by Dirichlet mixing of real single cells.
#'
#' @param counts Genes x cells count matrix.
#' @param cell_types Character vector of cell type labels (length = ncol(counts)).
#' @param n_samples Number of pseudobulk samples.
#' @param n_cells_per_sample Cells per mixture.
#' @param alpha Dirichlet concentration (1.0 = uniform, <1.0 = sparser).
#' @param seed Random seed.
#' @return List with bulk_counts (genes x samples) and ground_truth (samples x types).
generate_pseudobulk <- function(counts, cell_types, n_samples = 200,
                                 n_cells_per_sample = 2000,
                                 alpha = 1.0, seed = 42) {
  set.seed(seed)
  ct_levels <- sort(unique(cell_types))
  n_types <- length(ct_levels)

  type_indices <- lapply(ct_levels, function(ct) which(cell_types == ct))
  names(type_indices) <- ct_levels

  count_mat <- as.matrix(counts)
  bulk <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(count_mat), ncol = n_samples)
  rownames(bulk) <- rownames(count_mat)
  proportions <- matrix(0, nrow = n_samples, ncol = n_types)

  for (i in seq_len(n_samples)) {
    props <- rgamma(n_types, shape = alpha)
    props <- props / sum(props)
    proportions[i, ] <- props

    cell_counts <- as.vector(rmultinom(1, n_cells_per_sample, props))
    sample_sum <- rep(0, nrow(count_mat))
    for (j in seq_along(ct_levels)) {
      if (cell_counts[j] == 0) next
      idx <- sample(type_indices[[ct_levels[j]]], cell_counts[j], replace = TRUE)
      sample_sum <- sample_sum + rowSums(count_mat[, idx, drop = FALSE])
    }
    bulk[, i] <- sample_sum
  }

  sample_names <- paste0("Bulk_", formatC(seq_len(n_samples), width = 4, flag = "0"))
  colnames(bulk) <- sample_names
  colnames(proportions) <- ct_levels
  gt <- data.frame(proportions, row.names = sample_names, check.names = FALSE)

  list(bulk_counts = bulk, ground_truth = gt)
}


#' Generate pseudobulk with fixed malignant fraction, Dirichlet for rest.
#'
#' @param counts Genes x cells count matrix.
#' @param cell_types Character vector of cell type labels.
#' @param mal_type Name of the malignant cell type.
#' @param n_samples Number of samples.
#' @param n_cells_per_sample Cells per mixture.
#' @param tumor_fractions Numeric vector of malignant fractions to sample from.
#' @param seed Random seed.
#' @return List with bulk_counts and ground_truth.
generate_pseudobulk_tumor <- function(counts, cell_types,
                                       mal_type = "Cancer Epithelial",
                                       n_samples = 200,
                                       n_cells_per_sample = 2000,
                                       tumor_fractions = seq(0.6, 0.9, 0.05),
                                       seed = 42) {
  set.seed(seed)
  ct_levels <- sort(unique(cell_types))
  nonmal_types <- setdiff(ct_levels, mal_type)
  all_types <- c(mal_type, nonmal_types)
  n_all <- length(all_types)

  type_indices <- lapply(ct_levels, function(ct) which(cell_types == ct))
  names(type_indices) <- ct_levels

  count_mat <- as.matrix(counts)
  bulk <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(count_mat), ncol = n_samples)
  rownames(bulk) <- rownames(count_mat)
  proportions <- matrix(0, nrow = n_samples, ncol = n_all)

  for (i in seq_len(n_samples)) {
    mal_frac <- sample(tumor_fractions, 1)
    nonmal_props <- rgamma(length(nonmal_types), shape = 1)
    nonmal_props <- nonmal_props / sum(nonmal_props) * (1 - mal_frac)

    props <- c(mal_frac, nonmal_props)
    proportions[i, ] <- props

    cell_counts <- as.vector(rmultinom(1, n_cells_per_sample, props))
    sample_sum <- rep(0, nrow(count_mat))
    for (j in seq_along(all_types)) {
      if (cell_counts[j] == 0) next
      idx <- sample(type_indices[[all_types[j]]], cell_counts[j], replace = TRUE)
      sample_sum <- sample_sum + rowSums(count_mat[, idx, drop = FALSE])
    }
    bulk[, i] <- sample_sum
  }

  sample_names <- paste0("Bulk_", formatC(seq_len(n_samples), width = 4, flag = "0"))
  colnames(bulk) <- sample_names
  colnames(proportions) <- all_types
  gt <- data.frame(proportions, row.names = sample_names, check.names = FALSE)

  list(bulk_counts = bulk, ground_truth = gt)
}

Generate 4 Scenarios 

# Scenario 1: Uniform (alpha=1.0)
pb_uniform <- generate_pseudobulk(
  sc_counts_test, sc_meta_test$cell_type,
  n_samples = 200, n_cells_per_sample = 2000, alpha = 1.0, seed = 42
)

# Scenario 2: Sparse (alpha=0.3)
pb_sparse <- generate_pseudobulk(
  sc_counts_test, sc_meta_test$cell_type,
  n_samples = 200, n_cells_per_sample = 2000, alpha = 0.3, seed = 43
)

# Scenario 3: Tumor purity (60-90%)
pb_tumor <- generate_pseudobulk_tumor(
  sc_counts_test, sc_meta_test$cell_type,
  mal_type = "Cancer Epithelial",
  n_samples = 200, n_cells_per_sample = 2000,
  tumor_fractions = seq(0.6, 0.9, 0.05), seed = 44
)

# Scenario 4: Titration (0-90%)
pb_titration <- generate_pseudobulk_tumor(
  sc_counts_test, sc_meta_test$cell_type,
  mal_type = "Cancer Epithelial",
  n_samples = 100, n_cells_per_sample = 2000,
  tumor_fractions = seq(0, 0.9, 0.1), seed = 45
)

scenarios <- list(
  uniform   = list(data = pb_uniform,   label = "Uniform (alpha=1.0)"),
  sparse    = list(data = pb_sparse,    label = "Sparse (alpha=0.3)"),
  tumor     = list(data = pb_tumor,     label = "Tumor Purity (60-90%)"),
  titration = list(data = pb_titration, label = "Titration (0-90%)")
)

for (nm in names(scenarios)) {
  cat(sprintf("  %s: %d samples\n", scenarios[[nm]]$label,
              ncol(scenarios[[nm]]$data$bulk_counts)))
}

Step 4: Cell Type Mapping

Wu et al. uses fine-grained cell type names. We collapse both ground truth and SpaCET estimates to a shared evaluation vocabulary.

# Wu et al. -> evaluation categories
WU_TO_EVAL <- c(
  "Cancer Epithelial" = "Malignant",
  "CAFs"              = "CAF",
  "Endothelial"       = "Endothelial",
  "T-cells"           = "T_cells",
  "B-cells"           = "B cell",
  "Plasmablasts"      = "Plasma",
  "Myeloid"           = "Myeloid",
  "PVL"               = "PVL",
  "Normal Epithelial" = "Normal_Epithelial"
)

# SpaCET fine types -> evaluation categories
SPACET_TO_EVAL <- c(
  "T CD4"            = "T_cells",
  "T CD8"            = "T_cells",
  "NK"               = "T_cells",
  "Macrophage"       = "Myeloid",
  "cDC"              = "Myeloid",
  "pDC"              = "Myeloid",
  "Macrophage other" = "Myeloid",
  "CAF"              = "CAF",
  "Endothelial"      = "Endothelial",
  "B cell"           = "B cell",
  "Plasma"           = "Plasma",
  "Mast"             = "Other",
  "Neutrophil"       = "Other",
  "Unidentifiable"   = "Other",
  "Malignant"        = "Malignant"
)


#' Collapse SpaCET propMat (types x samples) to evaluation categories.
collapse_spacet_propmat <- function(prop_mat) {
  eval_types <- unique(SPACET_TO_EVAL)
  eval_types <- setdiff(eval_types, "Other")  # drop "Other" from evaluation

  result <- matrix(0, nrow = length(eval_types), ncol = ncol(prop_mat))
  rownames(result) <- eval_types
  colnames(result) <- colnames(prop_mat)

  for (spacet_type in rownames(prop_mat)) {
    eval_type <- SPACET_TO_EVAL[spacet_type]
    if (!is.na(eval_type) && eval_type %in% eval_types) {
      result[eval_type, ] <- result[eval_type, ] + prop_mat[spacet_type, ]
    }
  }
  as.data.frame(t(result))  # samples x types
}


#' Collapse ground truth from Wu names to evaluation categories.
collapse_ground_truth <- function(gt) {
  eval_types <- unique(WU_TO_EVAL)
  eval_types <- setdiff(eval_types, c("PVL", "Normal_Epithelial"))  # drop minor

  result <- data.frame(matrix(0, nrow = nrow(gt), ncol = length(eval_types)))
  colnames(result) <- eval_types
  rownames(result) <- rownames(gt)

  for (wu_type in colnames(gt)) {
    eval_type <- WU_TO_EVAL[wu_type]
    if (!is.na(eval_type) && eval_type %in% eval_types) {
      result[, eval_type] <- result[, eval_type] + gt[, wu_type]
    }
  }
  result
}

Step 5: SpaCET Deconvolution with Matched scRNAseq (TRAIN Set)

Build the reference from the TRAIN split and deconvolve pseudobulk.

# Prepare sc_annotation (cellID, cellType)
sc_annotation <- data.frame(
  cellID   = colnames(sc_counts_train),
  cellType = sc_meta_train$cell_type,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
rownames(sc_annotation) <- sc_annotation$cellID

# Lineage tree for Wu et al. cell types (flat -- each type is its own lineage)
sc_lineageTree <- as.list(setNames(
  unique(sc_meta_train$cell_type),
  unique(sc_meta_train$cell_type)
))

# Helper: wrap bulk counts in SpaCET object
make_bulk_spacet <- function(bulk_counts) {
  coords <- data.frame(X = seq_len(ncol(bulk_counts)), Y = rep(1, ncol(bulk_counts)))
  rownames(coords) <- colnames(bulk_counts)
  create.SpaCET.object(counts = bulk_counts, spotCoordinates = coords, platform = "OldST")
}

# Run deconvolution on each scenario
spacet_results <- list()

for (nm in names(scenarios)) {
  cat(sprintf("\nDeconvolving %s...\n", scenarios[[nm]]$label))
  obj <- make_bulk_spacet(scenarios[[nm]]$data$bulk_counts)

  obj <- SpaCET.deconvolution.matched.scRNAseq(
    obj,
    sc_includeMalignant  = TRUE,
    cancerType           = "BRCA",
    sc_counts            = sc_counts_train,
    sc_annotation        = sc_annotation,
    sc_lineageTree       = sc_lineageTree,
    sc_downsampling      = TRUE,
    sc_nCellEachLineage  = 200,
    coreNo               = 1
  )

  pm <- obj@results$deconvolution$propMat
  spacet_results[[nm]] <- collapse_spacet_propmat(pm)
  cat(sprintf("  propMat: %d types x %d samples\n", nrow(pm), ncol(pm)))
}

Step 6: Export for MuSiC

Export the TRAIN set reference and pseudobulk for running MuSiC externally.

output_dir <- "outputs/t8_real"
dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Subsample train set for manageable export (max 500 per type)
set.seed(99)
sub_idx <- unlist(lapply(unique(sc_meta_train$cell_type), function(ct) {
  idx <- which(sc_meta_train$cell_type == ct)
  sample(idx, min(500, length(idx)))
}))
sc_counts_export <- sc_counts_train[, sub_idx]
sc_meta_export   <- sc_meta_train[sub_idx, ]

# scRNA-seq reference
write.csv(as.matrix(sc_counts_export), file.path(output_dir, "sc_counts.csv"))
write.csv(
  data.frame(cell_type = sc_meta_export$cell_type,
             subject_id = sc_meta_export$subject_id,
             row.names = colnames(sc_counts_export)),
  file.path(output_dir, "sc_meta.csv")
)

# Pseudobulk + ground truth for each scenario
for (nm in names(scenarios)) {
  write.csv(scenarios[[nm]]$data$bulk_counts,
            file.path(output_dir, paste0("bulk_", nm, ".csv")))
  write.csv(scenarios[[nm]]$data$ground_truth,
            file.path(output_dir, paste0("gt_", nm, ".csv")))
}

# MuSiC runner script
music_script <- '
library(MuSiC)
library(Biobase)

sc_counts <- as.matrix(read.csv("sc_counts.csv", row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE))
sc_meta   <- read.csv("sc_meta.csv", row.names = 1)
sc_pheno  <- new("AnnotatedDataFrame", data = sc_meta)
sc_eset   <- ExpressionSet(assayData = sc_counts, phenoData = sc_pheno)

for (scenario in c("uniform", "sparse", "tumor", "titration")) {
  cat(sprintf("Running MuSiC for %s...\\n", scenario))
  bulk <- as.matrix(read.csv(paste0("bulk_", scenario, ".csv"),
                              row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE))
  bulk_eset <- ExpressionSet(assayData = bulk)
  result <- music_prop(bulk.eset = bulk_eset, sc.eset = sc_eset,
                        clusters = "cell_type", verbose = TRUE)
  write.csv(result$Est.prop.weighted,
            paste0("music_", scenario, ".csv"))
}
cat("Done.\\n")
'
writeLines(music_script, file.path(output_dir, "run_music.R"))
cat("MuSiC export written to", output_dir, "\n")

Step 7: Evaluate 

#' Evaluate deconvolution: overall + per-type Pearson r, RMSE.
evaluate <- function(est_df, gt_df, label) {
  common <- intersect(colnames(est_df), colnames(gt_df))
  common_samples <- intersect(rownames(est_df), rownames(gt_df))

  if (length(common) == 0 || length(common_samples) == 0) {
    warning(paste0("No common types/samples for ", label))
    return(list(pearson_r = NA, spearman_rho = NA, rmse = NA, per_type = NULL))
  }

  est_aligned <- as.matrix(est_df[common_samples, common])
  gt_aligned  <- as.matrix(gt_df[common_samples, common])

  e_flat <- as.vector(est_aligned)
  g_flat <- as.vector(gt_aligned)

  pearson_r   <- cor(e_flat, g_flat, method = "pearson")
  spearman_rho <- cor(e_flat, g_flat, method = "spearman")
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((e_flat - g_flat)^2))

  per_type <- data.frame(
    cell_type = common,
    pearson_r = sapply(common, function(ct) {
      if (sd(gt_aligned[, ct]) < 1e-15) return(NA)
      cor(est_aligned[, ct], gt_aligned[, ct])
    }),
    rmse = sapply(common, function(ct) {
      sqrt(mean((est_aligned[, ct] - gt_aligned[, ct])^2))
    }),
    n = length(common_samples),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE, row.names = NULL
  )

  list(pearson_r = pearson_r, spearman_rho = spearman_rho,
       rmse = rmse, per_type = per_type)
}

# Evaluate SpaCET
spacet_metrics <- list()
for (nm in names(scenarios)) {
  gt_eval <- collapse_ground_truth(scenarios[[nm]]$data$ground_truth)
  m <- evaluate(spacet_results[[nm]], gt_eval, nm)
  spacet_metrics[[nm]] <- m
  cat(sprintf("%-25s  r=%.4f  rho=%.4f  RMSE=%.4f\n",
              scenarios[[nm]]$label, m$pearson_r, m$spearman_rho, m$rmse))
}

Load MuSiC Results (if available) 

music_metrics <- list()
has_music <- FALSE

for (nm in names(scenarios)) {
  music_file <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("music_", nm, ".csv"))
  if (!file.exists(music_file)) next

  has_music <- TRUE
  music_props <- read.csv(music_file, row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
  # Collapse to evaluation categories
  music_eval <- collapse_ground_truth(music_props)  # same mapping
  gt_eval <- collapse_ground_truth(scenarios[[nm]]$data$ground_truth)

  m <- evaluate(music_eval, gt_eval, paste0("music_", nm))
  music_metrics[[nm]] <- m
  cat(sprintf("MuSiC %-20s  r=%.4f\n", scenarios[[nm]]$label, m$pearson_r))
}

if (!has_music) {
  cat("MuSiC results not found. Run: Rscript outputs/t8_real/run_music.R\n")
}

Step 8: Summary Table 

summary_rows <- list()
for (nm in names(scenarios)) {
  row <- data.frame(
    Scenario = scenarios[[nm]]$label,
    SpaCET_r = round(spacet_metrics[[nm]]$pearson_r, 4),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  if (has_music && nm %in% names(music_metrics)) {
    row$MuSiC_r <- round(music_metrics[[nm]]$pearson_r, 4)
  }
  summary_rows[[nm]] <- row
}
summary_table <- do.call(rbind, summary_rows)
rownames(summary_table) <- NULL
print(summary_table)

Step 9: Figures

9a. Scatter Plots (per scenario) 

plot_scatter <- function(est_df, gt_df, title_str, pearson_r) {
  common <- intersect(colnames(est_df), colnames(gt_df))
  common_samples <- intersect(rownames(est_df), rownames(gt_df))
  colors <- rainbow(length(common), s = 0.7, v = 0.85)

  plot(NULL, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1),
       xlab = "Ground Truth Fraction", ylab = "Predicted Fraction",
       main = title_str)
  abline(0, 1, lty = 2, col = "gray40")

  for (i in seq_along(common)) {
    ct <- common[i]
    points(gt_df[common_samples, ct], est_df[common_samples, ct],
           col = adjustcolor(colors[i], alpha.f = 0.5), pch = 16, cex = 0.6)
  }
  legend("topleft", legend = common, col = colors, pch = 16,
         cex = 0.55, ncol = 2, bty = "n")
  text(0.95, 0.05, sprintf("r = %.3f", pearson_r), adj = 1, cex = 1.1)
}

par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))
for (nm in names(scenarios)) {
  gt_eval <- collapse_ground_truth(scenarios[[nm]]$data$ground_truth)
  plot_scatter(spacet_results[[nm]], gt_eval,
               paste0("SpaCET - ", scenarios[[nm]]$label),
               spacet_metrics[[nm]]$pearson_r)
}
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

9b. Per-Cell-Type Accuracy 

# Combine per-type metrics across scenarios
all_per_type <- list()
for (nm in names(scenarios)) {
  pt <- spacet_metrics[[nm]]$per_type
  pt$scenario <- scenarios[[nm]]$label
  all_per_type[[nm]] <- pt
}
per_type_df <- do.call(rbind, all_per_type)

# Bar plot
par(mar = c(5, 9, 4, 2))
cell_types_eval <- unique(per_type_df$cell_type)
scenario_labels <- sapply(scenarios, function(s) s$label)
colors_scen <- c("#E64B35", "#4DBBD5", "#00A087", "#F39B7F")

n_types <- length(cell_types_eval)
n_scen  <- length(scenario_labels)
bar_mat <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_scen, ncol = n_types)

for (i in seq_along(scenario_labels)) {
  sub <- per_type_df[per_type_df$scenario == scenario_labels[i], ]
  for (j in seq_along(cell_types_eval)) {
    idx <- which(sub$cell_type == cell_types_eval[j])
    if (length(idx) > 0) bar_mat[i, j] <- sub$pearson_r[idx]
  }
}

barplot(bar_mat, beside = TRUE, horiz = TRUE, names.arg = cell_types_eval,
        col = colors_scen, las = 1, xlab = "Pearson r",
        main = "Per-cell-type accuracy by scenario",
        xlim = c(min(0, min(bar_mat, na.rm = TRUE) - 0.1), 1))
legend("bottomright", legend = scenario_labels, fill = colors_scen, cex = 0.7, bty = "n")
abline(v = 0, col = "black", lwd = 0.5)
par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2))

9c. Method Comparison (SpaCET vs MuSiC) 

if (has_music) {
  scenario_labels <- sapply(scenarios, function(s) s$label)
  spacet_rs <- sapply(names(scenarios), function(nm) spacet_metrics[[nm]]$pearson_r)
  music_rs  <- sapply(names(scenarios), function(nm) {
    if (nm %in% names(music_metrics)) music_metrics[[nm]]$pearson_r else NA
  })

  bar_mat <- rbind(SpaCET = spacet_rs, MuSiC = music_rs)
  colnames(bar_mat) <- scenario_labels

  par(mar = c(8, 4, 4, 2))
  bp <- barplot(bar_mat, beside = TRUE, col = c("#3b82f6", "#f97316"),
                ylim = c(0, 1.1), las = 2,
                main = "Deconvolution Benchmark - Fair Subject-Split\n(Wu et al. BRCA)",
                ylab = "Pearson r (overall)")
  legend("topright", legend = c("SpaCET", "MuSiC"),
         fill = c("#3b82f6", "#f97316"), bty = "n")

  # Add r values on top of bars
  text(bp, bar_mat + 0.03, sprintf("%.2f", bar_mat), cex = 0.7, adj = 0.5)
  par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2))
}

Session Info 

sessionInfo()
